Law Navi

Disclaimer & Terms of Use

Please read this before relying on anything in Law Navi. By using this application you acknowledge and agree to the following.

Scope & legality

Law Navi is general legal information published for public education — the kind of explanation of the law that anyone may write and share as protected speech. Explaining, mapping, and organizing the law for the general public is not the unauthorized practice of law. Law Navi does not apply the law to your specific situation, give personalized recommendations, draft legal documents for you, or represent anyone. The Theory Builder is a general educational study and note-organizing aid — not legal services and not legal advice. Unauthorized-practice rules vary by state; nothing here is offered as legal services in any jurisdiction.

Not legal advice

Law Navi is a free educational and informational resource that presents general information about United States law. It is not legal advice and must not be relied upon as legal advice. It does not address your specific facts or circumstances, and using it cannot substitute for advice from a licensed attorney.

The author is not a lawyer

The author and contributors are not attorneys, are not licensed to practice law in any jurisdiction, and are not holding themselves out as lawyers. Nothing here is provided by a lawyer or in the course of an attorney-client relationship.

No attorney-client relationship

Using Law Navi, contacting the author, or building a "theory" in the Theory Builder does not create an attorney-client relationship of any kind. No confidential or fiduciary relationship is formed.

No personalized legal advice; not the practice of law

Law Navi provides general legal information for self-education. It does not apply the law to your particular situation, does not recommend a course of action, and is not intended as the practice of law or the provision of personalized legal services. The Theory Builder is a general note-organizing and educational drafting aid; any analysis, evaluation, or AI-generated suggestion it produces is a study aid only, not a legal opinion, and may be wrong.

Accuracy, currency & AI limitations

The law changes constantly and varies by jurisdiction. Content here may be incomplete, outdated, oversimplified, or simply incorrect. Portions were generated with AI and may contain errors or fabricated citations. Verify everything against primary sources and current law before relying on it.

No warranty

Law Navi is provided "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE," without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy, and non-infringement, to the fullest extent permitted by law.

Limitation of liability

To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the author and contributors shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, or any loss arising out of or related to your access to, use of, or reliance on Law Navi or its content — even if advised of the possibility of such damages. You use it entirely at your own risk.

Consult a licensed attorney

For any actual legal matter, consult a licensed attorney in your jurisdiction. If you cannot afford one, contact your state or local bar association's lawyer-referral service or a legal-aid organization.

Your data stays local

Theories you build are stored in your browser (optionally encrypted), and any AI features connect only to a local model endpoint you configure. Law Navi does not transmit your data to the author or any cloud service. You are responsible for safeguarding your own data and passphrases.

Law Navi
Navigate the entire law of the United States, in plain English

Every level — federal, state by state, local, the territories, tribal nations, and even the law that applies on the ocean, in the air, and in space — plus the theories, the live battlegrounds, the procedure, and the exploits people use to bend the system. Then build and test your own legal theory. We begin where America begins: the Constitution.
